name: Charleston

confRecord.note: 5-8-3 overall, 2-3-2 conf. Missed CAA Tourn. Hofstra won 4th consecutive CAA title.

notable: Battery connections provide occasional USL pathways
proPlayers.draftRank: Limited MLS pipeline; local USL Battery connections

acuAlignNote: Core kinesiology covered. Charleston's program is solid but smaller than major research universities. Good for a student who prioritises lifestyle and city over research facilities.

gpa.note: College of Charleston is accessible. GPA 2.8 is comfortable for both admission and aid.

fin.internationalNote: Affordable D1. Athletic scholarship can cover most costs. Academic merit available for 3.0+ GPA.

culture.vibe: One of America's most beautiful and charming cities. The College of Charleston sits in the heart of the historic district — cobblestone streets, antebellum architecture, and waterfront beauty. The campus atmosphere matches the city — relaxed, social, and genuinely enjoyable.
culture.thingsToDo: Rainbow Row architecture walks, Sullivan's Island and Isle of Palms beaches (20 min), Charleston Battery waterfront, King Street shopping and restaurants, Fort Sumter tours, Mount Pleasant Pier, Boone Hall Plantation. Charleston is endlessly photogenic.
culture.socialScene: Warm, Southern hospitality. Predominately domestic students but growing international presence. Charleston Battery soccer connection means players have local pro visibility. Beach access excellent. Social scene on Upper King Street is one of the best college bar strips in the South.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Charleston is a genuinely beautiful and liveable city. Warm, coastal, social — closer to Sydney lifestyle than most East Coast options. Smaller D1 program means personal attention. Excellent city culture.

facilityDetails.stadium: Patriots Point Soccer Complex — opened 2000, $6.5M facility, 5,000 capacity, natural grass, lights. Located 7 miles from main campus at Mt. Pleasant with stunning Charleston Harbour views from the pitch.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Training fields at Patriots Point and additional on-campus practice areas. Natural grass maintained to good standard. Year-round Charleston climate allows outdoor training 11+ months annually.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: CofC Athletics Weight Room — 8,000 sq ft, functional, adequate. Free weights, Olympic platforms, cable systems. Shared with all CofC varsity sports. Smaller program means more individual attention from S&C staff. Access to Charleston Battery training facilities occasionally available for top players.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) introduced 2023. Video analysis via Hudl. Kinesiology department has basic exercise physiology and biomechanics teaching labs. Less sophisticated sports science infrastructure than larger programs but growing.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CofC Athletics certified athletic trainer. Medical University of South Carolina (MUSC) sports medicine partnership — MUSC is a top academic medical centre with strong orthopaedics and sports cardiology. Good medical access for a mid-major D1 program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: CofC Department of Health and Human Performance: biomechanics teaching lab, exercise physiology lab, motor behaviour lab. MUSC clinical placement connections for pre-PT students are valuable — MUSC has a Doctor of Physical Therapy program on campus.
facilityDetails.extras: Patriots Point Naval Museum adjacent to soccer complex (USS Yorktown aircraft carrier). Charleston Harbour and beach access. Historic King Street and Market Street in downtown Charleston. Battery soccer facility has the best views of any soccer stadium in this guide — Charleston Harbour waterfront.
facilityDetails.note: Patriots Point is a gem — 5,000 cap natural grass with medical training building and harbour views. Off-campus location (7 miles) is the main knock against an otherwise strong mid-major facility. MUSC DPT partnership is a significant pre-PT asset.

coach.name: Keith Wiggans
coach.title: Head Coach (6th season 2025)
coach.profile: 6th season as HC 2025. Overall record: 27-31-18, CAA: 10-19-8. 2024 best season: 10-6-2, ranked #23 nationally Sep 2024. CofC alum (2000-04) — holds school records for career GAA (0.99), wins (41), shutouts (23), saves (269). Played 7 seasons for Charleston Battery (USL). Was assistant/assoc HC under Ralph Lundy for 11 seasons before taking over 2019.

rec: Charleston is one of America's most beautiful cities — historic waterfront, warm climate. Keith Wiggans played for the local pro team (Charleston Battery). Best if Olivier values city lifestyle and a developing D1 program with personal coaching.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1 competitive: 4 rising juniors present. Yr2 Olivier proves himself while juniors become seniors. Yr3 major clearance opens leadership role.

staff.name: Jet Terry
staff.bg: Hired March 2025. Charleston native, former CofC player 2016. 2 seasons as assistant at Saginaw Valley State (D2) where he helped win conf title and reach NCAA QF. Previously Director of Soccer Operations at CofC 2022-23.

staff.name: Ernesto Aguinaga-Hernandez
staff.bg: Joined July 2025. Previously volunteer assistant at Univ of Wisconsin-Parkside 4 seasons. Helped 2 conference titles, 3 NCAA D2 tournaments. Former trialist Club Leon FC (Mexico).

name: William & Mary
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology and health sciences pathway. Pre-PT advising available. Williamsburg is near Sentara Healthcare for clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: William & Mary out-of-state COA is ~$73k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Williamsburg, VA has moderate cost of living. One of the most selective public universities in the US.

gpa.note: Selective public liberal arts college. Strong academic profile required.

confRecord.note: 1-6-1 in CAA South division. Bottom of south table.
3-4-1 conf record. Mid-table finish in 11-team CAA.
1-4-3 conf record. Bottom of 11-team CAA standings.

coach.name: Shaun Tsakiris
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Shaun Tsakiris is one of the longest-tenured coaches in the CAA — building W&M consistently as a competitive conference program. Small campus with strong academic community. Sentara Healthcare provides pre-PT clinical access. GPA 3.0+ required — above Olivier's current level.

rec: Best academic profile in the CAA. Small colonial campus. Consistently competitive program — good for development path.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 3 of 7 MFs clear before Olivier arrives (Sr×2, Gr×1). 4 remain including 2 rising juniors — competitive but manageable for a quality recruit.

facilityDetails.stadium: Martin Family Stadium, Williamsburg, VA. Dedicated on-campus soccer stadium. Historic campus setting.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: W&M Athletic Complex. Training fields on historic campus grounds.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: W&M Athletics S&C facility. Full-time staff for D1 athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology labs — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Pre-PT advising available.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Sentara Healthcare (major regional system, 12+ hospitals) — pre-PT clinical access. Williamsburg area hospitals accessible.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: W&M Kinesiology and Health Sciences labs. Good ACU alignment for core units.
facilityDetails.extras: Williamsburg historic district — one of the most beautiful small cities in the US. Colonial Williamsburg, Busch Gardens, beaches 60 miles east. DC accessible (2.5 hr). Norfolk MLS expansion rumours. Virginia Beach nearby.
facilityDetails.note: One of the most selective public universities in the US. GPA 3.0+ required — currently above Olivier's level. Strong academic reputation.

culture.vibe: Small selective public liberal arts college with strong traditions. Close-knit campus, high academic standards, colonial campus setting. Athletic culture is serious without being overwhelmingly football-dominated.
culture.thingsToDo: Colonial Williamsburg (historic), Busch Gardens (theme park), Virginia Beach (1 hr), DC (2.5 hr), Blue Ridge Mountains.
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit academic community. Greek life present but not dominant. Small campus = strong team bonds.
culture.olivierMatch: Academic profile is aspirational for Olivier (GPA 3.0+ required). But the soccer program is genuinely competitive in the CAA — development pathway is strong. Non-warm, non-city campus — low lifestyle fit for Olivier's Sydney preferences.
culture.lifestyleTags: Cool climate, small town, selective, academic, historic campus

staff.name: Shaun Tsakiris
staff.bg: Long-tenured W&M head coach. One of the most experienced coaches in the CAA. Program consistently competitive under his tenure.

name: Hofstra
acuAlignNote: Health Sciences covers anatomy, physiology. NYC proximity (30 min via LIRR) opens extensive clinical placement for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Hofstra full COA is ~$62k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Long Island suburban campus with proximity to NYC — moderate-to-high living costs. Strong sports science and health programs.

gpa.note: Private university with flexible admission. Athletic scholarships standard.

confRecord.note: 4th consecutive CAA Tournament title. 9th NCAA appearance. Beat UNCW in final.
3rd consecutive CAA Tournament title. No. 7 NCAA seed.

coach.name: Scott Coufal
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Scott Coufal has built Hofstra into the dominant CAA force — 4 consecutive CAA Tournament titles (2022-25). NYC proximity via LIRR (30 min to Penn Station) is an exceptional recruitment advantage. CAA's elite program for development pathway.

rec: NYC proximity via LIRR is the main appeal. Solid CAA program. Expensive for the tier.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Only 2 of 6 MFs clear. Heavy rebuild load with 3 rising juniors (Jr by 2027) — Olivier would enter a crowded midfield. Note: James York (#14) is Australian from Melbourne.

facilityDetails.stadium: Hofstra Soccer Stadium, Hempstead, NY. On-campus dedicated soccer facility. 4x CAA champion facilities.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Hofstra Athletic Complex. Long Island suburban campus with good training infrastructure.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Hofstra D1 S&C facility. Full-time athletic training staff.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Health Sciences labs. NYC proximity opens research collaboration with major medical institutions.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: NYU Langone Health, Northwell Health (on Long Island) — premier pre-PT clinical access. NYC medical corridor is extraordinary.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Sciences labs covering anatomy, physiology. NYC hospital rotations possible for motivated pre-PT students.
facilityDetails.extras: NYC via LIRR (30 min). Madison Square Garden, NYCFC, Red Bulls (MLS). Long Island beaches. Excellent transport links. Most cosmopolitan D1 location in the CAA at this cost point.
facilityDetails.note: Dominant CAA program — 4 consecutive titles. NYC access is major lifestyle and clinical asset. Cost ($62k) is high for this tier — scholarship essential.

culture.vibe: Suburban Long Island with exceptional NYC access. Campus is neat and modern. Soccer culture is strong — Hofstra is the conference's elite program and players know it. NYCFC/Red Bulls access adds professional culture.
culture.thingsToDo: NYC 30 min by LIRR — all of Manhattan's culture, food, sport. Long Island beaches (30 min). Jones Beach, The Hamptons. NYCFC and Red Bulls MLS.
culture.socialScene: Suburban private university — active social scene but campus-contained. Strong athletic culture. NYC nightlife accessible.
culture.olivierMatch: Best CAA soccer development program — 4 consecutive titles shows elite conference environment. NYC clinical access is excellent for pre-PT. Cost is the main barrier ($62k → scholarship essential). Not warm, not classic city campus, but NYC proximity compensates.
culture.lifestyleTags: NYC access, CAA champion, Long Island, not warm, high cost

staff.name: Scott Coufal
staff.bg: 4 consecutive CAA Tournament titles (2022-25). Built Hofstra into the CAA's dominant program. NYC market recruiter with international student experience.

name: Northeastern
acuAlignNote: Strong health sciences and neuroscience pathways. Co-op program provides real clinical placement in Boston hospital system — excellent for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Northeastern full COA is ~$78k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Co-op program provides paid work placements that can significantly offset costs over the degree. Merit aid available. Boston living costs are high.

gpa.note: Selective private university with strong co-op placement. Athletic scholarship available.

confRecord.note: 2-5-1 in CAA North division. Mid-lower table.
5-2-1 conf record in 9-team CAA. Strong finish.

coach.name: Brian Ainscough
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Brian Ainscough has built Northeastern as a competitive CAA program in one of the US's premier academic cities. The co-op model provides paid clinical work placements in Boston's world-class hospital network — unmatched pre-PT pathway. GPA 3.0+ required — above Olivier's current level.

courses: Co-op Term — Boston Hospital Clinical Placement (paid)
rec: Boston city + co-op clinical placement in hospitals = outstanding pre-PT pathway. Expensive but co-op offsets some cost. Soccer is mid-CAA.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: ⚠️ 2025 roster (+1 applied). Massive 10/12 MF turnover — only 2 Freshmen remain by Fall 2027. Elite opportunity window. Northeastern's co-op model means fewer players per year.

facilityDetails.stadium: Parsons Field, Brookline, MA. Northeastern home field in the Boston area. Urban campus facilities.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Northeastern Urban campus facilities. Access to public Boston parks and Boston-area training pitches.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Northeastern D1 S&C facility. Full-time athletic training staff.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Boston is the world's leading biomedical research hub — Brigham & Women's, MGH, Boston Children's. Co-op placements in sports science available.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Massachusetts General Hospital, Brigham & Women's, Boston Children's Hospital — co-op program provides paid clinical rotations. Best pre-PT access in the entire guide (tied with UAB).
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Sciences and Behavioral Neuroscience labs. Co-op model: up to 3 paid work terms in relevant clinical settings alongside soccer.
facilityDetails.extras: Boston — one of the greatest sports cities in the US. New England Revolution (MLS). Harvard, MIT, BU all within 2 miles. Charles River Esplanade. Freedom Trail. Incredible academic culture.
facilityDetails.note: Best pre-PT clinical access in the CAA via co-op program. GPA 3.0+ required and cost is very high ($78k). Co-op paid terms can meaningfully offset net cost over 5 years.

culture.vibe: Major urban private research university in the heart of Boston. Campus is integrated into the city — Fenway Park is a 5-min walk. Strong academic culture, co-op program means students are professionals by graduation. Soccer is competitive but not Hofstra-level.
culture.thingsToDo: Boston — Fenway Park, TD Garden, Freedom Trail, Charles River, Boston Common. New England Revolution MLS. Harvard Square. All within walking/T distance.
culture.socialScene: Urban private university social scene. Boston has one of the best college social cultures in the US — 50+ universities within 20 miles. Co-op rotations take students out of campus 2-3 terms — unique experience.
culture.olivierMatch: Academically aspirational (GPA 3.0+, cost $78k). But the co-op pre-PT pathway and Boston environment are extraordinary. Listed as aspirational — if GPA improves and scholarship available, Northeastern would be top CAA academic option.
culture.lifestyleTags: Boston city, co-op clinical, cold winters, elite academic, aspirational cost

staff.name: Brian Ainscough
staff.bg: Northeastern head coach — building CAA competitive program in Boston. Co-op model and Boston medical corridor are key selling points for pre-PT track athletes.

name: Drexel
acuAlignNote: Health Sciences covers anatomy, physiology. Co-op program provides clinical placements in Philadelphia hospital network for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Drexel full COA is ~$86k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Co-op program provides paid work placements that can offset costs meaningfully. Merit aid available. Philadelphia cost of living is moderate for a major US city.

gpa.note: Selective private university. Co-op model reduces overall cost over 5-year program.

coach.name: David Castellanos
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Drexel's co-op model provides paid clinical placements in Philadelphia's hospital network — Jefferson Health, Penn Medicine, CHOP. 2021 CAA Champions — program has genuine upside. Philadelphia is an affordable major city with Union (MLS) access.

courses: Co-op Term — Philadelphia Hospital Clinical Placement (paid)
rec: Philadelphia city + co-op clinical placements. Similar to Northeastern but in Philly. Soccer is lower-tier CAA.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 12 MFs clear — but 7 remain across Year 1 and 2. Deep MF corps makes early minutes competitive. Opportunity improves Year 3+ as classes turn over.

facilityDetails.stadium: Vidas Athletic Complex, Philadelphia, PA. Drexel soccer facilities on urban campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Drexel Athletic Complex. Urban campus training options with access to Fairmount Park fields.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Drexel Athletics S&C facility. Full-time training staff.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Health Sciences labs. Co-op model enables sports science clinical placements.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Jefferson Health, Penn Medicine, CHOP (Children's Hospital of Philadelphia) — paid co-op rotations in clinical settings. Excellent pre-PT access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Sciences labs for anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Co-op clinical terms in Philadelphia hospital network.
facilityDetails.extras: Philadelphia Union (MLS) — 15 min from campus. Fairmount Park, Center City, South Street. Liberty Bell, Constitution Center. Moderate cost of living for a major city. NYC accessible (1.5 hr by train).
facilityDetails.note: 2021 CAA Champions — program has proven it can compete at top of conference. Co-op clinical + Philadelphia Union access is strong combination.

culture.vibe: Urban private research university with co-op model. Campus integrates into Philadelphia's University City neighborhood alongside Penn and Temple. Dragon culture is passionate — 2021 CAA championship still resonates.
culture.thingsToDo: Philadelphia Union (MLS), Center City Philadelphia, Reading Terminal Market, South Street, Fairmount Park, Springsteen's home town (he's a fan). NYC by train (1.5 hr).
culture.socialScene: University City neighborhood — Penn, Drexel, UPenn all adjacent. Active social scene. Co-op model means students rotate out of campus — diverse experiences.
culture.olivierMatch: Borderline GPA fit (2.8+). Co-op clinical in Philadelphia hospital network + Union MLS proximity is solid pre-PT pathway. Cost ($65k) is high — scholarship essential. 2021 CAA champions shows program quality.
culture.lifestyleTags: Philadelphia city, co-op clinical, Union MLS, not warm, moderate cost

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Drexel Dragons head coach. 2021 CAA Champions program. Philadelphia co-op model provides paid clinical placements for pre-PT track athletes.

name: Delaware
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology and applied physiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science well-covered. Pre-PT pathway supported.

fin.internationalNote: Delaware out-of-state COA is ~$38k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 40–60% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. Newark, DE has moderate cost of living. Strong academic reputation and health science programs.

gpa.note: Public university with strong academics. Athletic scholarship pathway established.

confRecord.note: Delaware left CAA for CUSA July 2025.

coach.name: Tommy McMenemy
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Delaware is a consistently competitive CAA program — strong kinesiology and applied physiology degree. Low cost ($38k out-of-state) with accessible GPA. Newark is a classic college town with Philly 25 min south. Note: Delaware moved from CAA to CUSA in July 2025.

rec: Strong kinesiology degree. Consistent CAA soccer. Low cost. Newark is a smaller college town — limited lifestyle.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Small 4-player MF pool — 2 clear, 2 remain (both So). Only 2 competitors in Year 1, suggesting real starting potential for a quality CMF like Olivier.

facilityDetails.stadium: Stuart & Suzanne Grant Stadium — 1,400 capacity, on-campus dedicated soccer complex in Newark, DE.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Delaware Athletic Complex. Full D1 training infrastructure on main campus.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Whitney Athletic Center — 10,000 sq ft S&C area, large training room with sports medicine facilities, kitchen and nutrition education space, auditorium. Built into Delaware Stadium (west side). Strong D1-level infrastructure.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology and Applied Physiology labs — strong research focus. Pre-PT pathway well-established.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Christiana Care Health System (major Wilmington/Newark regional hospital) — pre-PT clinical access. Philadelphia hospitals 25 min north.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology and Applied Physiology labs. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science all well-covered.
facilityDetails.extras: Philadelphia 25 min by SEPTA. Union (MLS) accessible. Newark College Town — affordable, contained. Delaware beaches 90 min. Washington DC 2 hr. Note: program now in CUSA (Conference USA) since July 2025 — conference label in this guide is CAA (historic).
facilityDetails.note: Whitney Athletic Center is the facility highlight — 10,000 sqft S&C + sports med is strong for a public mid-major. Conference change: Delaware moved to CUSA July 2025. Listed under CAA in this guide (historical/legacy).

culture.vibe: Classic large public university college town. University of Delaware dominates Newark, DE — it's a college town built around the university. Blue Hen pride is strong. Soccer culture competitive.
culture.thingsToDo: Philadelphia (25 min SEPTA), Delaware beaches (90 min), DC (2 hr). Newark Downtown — bars, restaurants, main street culture. Philadelphia Union MLS.
culture.socialScene: Large public university scene — Greek life, sports culture, college town bars and restaurants on Main Street, Newark.
culture.olivierMatch: Borderline GPA (2.5+). Best value CAA option for Olivier — low cost, strong kinesiology degree, competitive soccer, Philly access. Non-warm campus, not city, but good value proposition. Note: now in CUSA.
culture.lifestyleTags: College town, low cost, strong kinesiology, Philly proximity, CUSA (2025+)

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Delaware Blue Hens head coach. Strong Applied Physiology program with competitive CAA (now CUSA) soccer history.

name: Elon
acuAlignNote: Exercise science covers core subjects. Pre-PT advising available. Small beautiful campus.

fin.internationalNote: Elon full COA is ~$48k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Merit scholarships available for international students with strong academic record. Small suburban campus in North Carolina with moderate cost of living.

gpa.note: Private university with merit+athletic scholarship opportunities.

confRecord.note: Won CAA South division and CAA Tournament title.
6-2 conf record. CAA regular season and tournament champions.
6-1-2 conf record. CAA champions — first title in program history.

coach.name: Marc Reeves
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Elon is the surprise CAA package — 2022 and 2025 CAA Tournament Champions from a small private university in rural North Carolina. Beautiful campus, strong community feel. Exercise Science program aligned with pre-PT. GPA borderline for Olivier.

facilities: Elon Soccer Complex — beautiful landscaped facility on Elon's award-winning campus
rec: Beautiful small campus. Rural North Carolina — low lifestyle fit. Mild climate is a marginal plus.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Large 17-player MF pool with 10 remaining by Fall 2027. Highly competitive environment — Olivier would need exceptional performance to break through. Opportunity grows Year 3+ as pool thins.

facilityDetails.stadium: Elon Soccer Complex — beautiful landscaped facility on Elon's award-winning campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Elon Athletic Complex. Small campus, intimate training environment.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science labs. Biomechanics, physiology, motor behaviour all covered.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Alamance Regional Medical Center (Burlington, adjacent to campus) — pre-PT clinical access. Cone Health network nearby.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs. Elon has a strong pre-professional advising culture — one of the best-advising small universities in the South.
facilityDetails.extras: Elon, NC is a small college town. Burlington, NC 5 min. Greensboro/Winston-Salem/Durham 30-45 min. Research Triangle (Raleigh-Durham-Chapel Hill — major biotech hub) 90 min. Charlotte 1.5 hr. Not warm/city but beautiful campus.
facilityDetails.note: 2022 + 2025 CAA Champions. Surprising elite-level soccer from a small private university. Best for Olivier if he wants a genuine title-contending soccer environment at mid-tier cost.

culture.vibe: Beautiful small private university campus consistently rated among the most beautiful in the US. Tight-knit community. Phoenix pride is strong — soccer wins CAA titles. Rural but with Greensboro and Research Triangle accessible.
culture.thingsToDo: Greensboro (30 min), Research Triangle (90 min), Charlotte (1.5 hr), Asheville (2 hr). North Carolina nature — hiking, camping. ACC basketball culture everywhere.
culture.socialScene: Small private university community. Greek life present. Very close team/campus bonds. Not a major party school — more outdoor/community-focused.
culture.olivierMatch: GPA borderline (2.5+). Not warm, not city — low lifestyle fit for Olivier's Sydney preferences. But 2022+2025 CAA titles shows genuine soccer program. Best if Olivier prioritises conference-title soccer over lifestyle.
culture.lifestyleTags: Beautiful campus, CAA champion, rural NC, not warm, not city

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Elon Phoenix head coach. 2022 + 2025 CAA Tournament Champions — program punches above its weight. Small private university with excellent campus culture.

name: Monmouth
acuAlignNote: Health studies covers core anatomy and physiology. NJ Shore location with NYC access for clinical placements.

fin.internationalNote: Monmouth full COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–50% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. NJ shore location with moderate-to-high living costs. Strong sports science pathway.

gpa.note: Accessible private university. Athletic scholarship packages available.

confRecord.note: 5-1-2 conf record. Strong finish, CAA Tournament run.
2-6-1 conf record in first CAA season after joining from Big South.
Not in CAA — joined 2022 from Big South Conference.
Not in CAA — joined 2022 from Big South Conference.

coach.name: Robert McCourt
coach.title: Head Coach (6th season)
coach.profile: Monmouth is a developing CAA program with a unique NJ Shore location. Best minutes opportunity for an international recruit at this conference level. Accessible GPA. NYC accessible (1 hr by train). Monmouth University Health Sciences provides pre-PT access.

facilities: Hesse Field on The Great Lawn, West Long Branch, NJ
rec: NJ Shore location has appeal — beach access, NYC day trips. Soccer and academics are developing tier. Best for maximum minutes opportunity.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: ⚠️ 2025 roster (+1 applied). Elite window — 4 of 5 MFs clear before Olivier arrives. Only 1 rising senior remains in Year 1. Monmouth actively rebuilding CAA program.

facilityDetails.stadium: Hesse Field on The Great Lawn, West Long Branch, NJ. On-campus FieldTurf surface (resurfaced 2014), Shore campus with ocean proximity.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Monmouth Athletic Complex. NJ Shore location with excellent outdoor training conditions spring/fall.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: OceanFirst Bank Center — fitness centre, 6-lane indoor track, 4,100-seat arena. Full S&C access for D1 athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Health Studies labs. Pre-PT pathway available.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Hackensack Meridian Health (major NJ health network) — pre-PT clinical access. Multiple hospitals within 20 min.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Studies labs for anatomy, physiology. Pre-PT advising available.
facilityDetails.extras: NJ Shore — Sandy Hook, Asbury Park, Long Branch beach. NYC by NJ Transit (1 hr). Red Bulls MLS in Harrison NJ (45 min). NYCFC and NY area soccer ecosystem. Boardwalk culture.
facilityDetails.note: Maximum minutes opportunity in the CAA. OceanFirst Bank Center provides solid S&C infrastructure. Shore location is lifestyle asset. NYC accessible. Developing program — best for Olivier if minutes are highest priority.

culture.vibe: Small private university with a distinctly NJ Shore vibe. Ocean 5 min from campus. Bruce Springsteen country — Asbury Park's music scene is famous. Campus is suburban but tight-knit. Soccer program building steadily in CAA.
culture.thingsToDo: NJ Shore beaches, Asbury Park, NYC by train (1 hr), Red Bulls MLS, Sandy Hook National Seashore. Shore boardwalk culture.
culture.socialScene: Small private university — active campus social scene. Shore-town vibe extends to campus culture. Athletic community is close.
culture.olivierMatch: GPA accessible (2.3+). NJ Shore lifestyle is unique. NYC accessible. But not warm (NJ winters), not city campus, developing CAA program. Maximum minutes opportunity makes it valuable as a minutes-first option.
culture.lifestyleTags: NJ Shore, NYC access, maximum minutes, developing, not warm

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Monmouth Hawks head coach. Developing CAA program. NJ Shore location and NYC proximity are key recruitment differentiators for international athletes.

name: Stony Brook
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Stony Brook Medicine is a top research medical centre — strong pre-PT access.

fin.internationalNote: Stony Brook out-of-state COA is ~$38k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 40–60% of COA. SUNY system rates are more affordable than private NY universities. Merit aid available. Long Island location with moderate-to-high living costs.

gpa.note: Public research university (SUNY). Strong academic profile for the cost.

confRecord.note: 4-3-1 in CAA North division. Strong recovery from 2024.
0-6-2 conf record. Winless in conference play.
3-6 conf record in first CAA season after joining from America East.
Not in CAA — joined 2022 from America East Conference.
Not in CAA — joined 2022 from America East Conference.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Stony Brook is a high-value CAA option — SUNY's flagship research university with on-campus Stony Brook Medicine (Level 1 trauma, nationally ranked). Low cost ($38k out-of-state). Strong bounce-back 2025 season after winless 2024. NYC by LIRR (1.5 hr).

rec: Strong value — low cost, research university, Stony Brook Medicine for pre-PT. Rural Long Island campus. NYC access via LIRR.

facilityDetails.stadium: Kenneth P. LaValle Stadium (football) shared use / Stony Brook Soccer Complex. Large SUNY campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Stony Brook Athletic Complex. Full D1 training infrastructure on large research campus.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Stony Brook Athletics S&C facility. Full-time staff.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Stony Brook Kinesiology labs. Strong research university sports science resources. Collaboration with Stony Brook Medicine possible.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Stony Brook Medicine — Level 1 trauma centre on campus. Top-tier pre-PT/Chiro clinical access. One of the best medical centres in the Northeast directly accessible to student athletes.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise science. SUNY research facilities are well-funded.
facilityDetails.extras: Stony Brook — Long Island. NYC by LIRR (1.5 hr). Stony Brook village, Smithtown, Port Jefferson harbour (walking distance). Hamptons 45 min. Stony Brook Medicine on campus. NYCFC/Red Bulls accessible.
facilityDetails.note: Best value in CAA: SUNY tuition ($27k) + Stony Brook Medicine on campus = outstanding value for pre-PT pathway. Long Island suburban/rural — not warm, not city.

culture.vibe: Large public research university. Campus is a small city unto itself — 26,000 students on a sprawling wooded Long Island campus. Soccer culture is building post-2022 CAA entry. Research university energy rather than college-town social scene.
culture.thingsToDo: Port Jefferson harbour (walking), LIRR to NYC (1.5 hr), Hamptons (45 min), Fire Island (30 min), Long Island wine country (30 min).
culture.socialScene: Large public research university. Diverse international student body. Campus social scene is active but dispersed across the large campus. NYC accessible for weekend culture.
culture.olivierMatch: Borderline GPA (2.5+). Best value pre-PT pathway in CAA — Stony Brook Medicine on campus + SUNY pricing + NYC accessible. Non-warm, Long Island suburban. 2025 bounce-back season shows program can compete. Strong value proposition for a budget-conscious Olivier.
culture.lifestyleTags: Best CAA value, Stony Brook Medicine, Long Island, NYC access, not warm

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: Stony Brook Seawolves head coach. Rebuilding CAA program at SUNY's flagship research university. Stony Brook Medicine on campus is exceptional pre-PT recruitment asset.
